title: Add GitHub sign-in and protect the application
objective: Require users to sign in with GitHub before they can access the artifact library, artifact details or protected application APIs.
userContext: The application will expose content stored in a private GitHub repository. Unauthenticated visitors must not be able to see artifact metadata or content. The user needs a simple GitHub-based sign-in experience that works in the Cloudflare-hosted application.
currentBehaviour: The application is publicly accessible and has no user authentication, session management, sign-in page or protected routes.
requiredBehaviour: Unauthenticated users must be redirected to a GitHub sign-in experience. After successful GitHub authentication, the application must establish a secure server-side session and allow access to protected pages and APIs. Users must be able to sign out. Authentication must work in the deployed Cloudflare Worker environment.
userExperience: An unauthenticated visitor sees a clear sign-in page with a Sign in with GitHub action. After authentication, the user returns to the application. Expired or invalid sessions return the user to sign-in without exposing protected content.
functionalRequirements: Implement the GitHub OAuth or GitHub App user authorisation flow.
Validate OAuth state to prevent request forgery.
Create a secure server-side session after successful authentication.
Store the session identifier in an HttpOnly, Secure and appropriately SameSite cookie.
Protect the library page, artifact detail pages and artifact APIs.
Ensure protected data is not included in responses for unauthenticated users.
Add sign-out behaviour that invalidates the session.
Handle denied authorisation, invalid callbacks and expired sessions clearly.
Support an optional return URL so users return to their intended page after sign-in.
Provide configuration validation for required GitHub and session secrets.
technicalConsiderations: The browser must not receive repository access credentials.
Authentication and session logic must run server-side.
The solution must work with Next.js, OpenNext and Cloudflare Workers.
Use encrypted or strongly random session identifiers.
Do not store secrets in committed files.
Use GitHub-provided identity as the initial user identity.
Keep repository authorisation separate for AUTH-002.
outOfScope: Verifying access to the private artifact repository.
Reading or writing artifacts from GitHub.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users cannot access the library page.
Unauthenticated users cannot access artifact detail pages or protected APIs.
The user can sign in through GitHub.
A successful callback creates a secure authenticated session.
Invalid or expired sessions are rejected.
No GitHub client secret or repository token is exposed to browser JavaScript.
Cloudflare-compatible tests and production build validation pass.
The current application specification is updated to describe authentication behaviour.
The implementation pull request includes AUTH-001 and closes the generated issue.
codexGuidance: Implement authentication only. Do not infer repository access from successful GitHub sign-in. Design the session abstraction so AUTH-002 can add repository authorisation without replacing the authentication flow.
